# Memory access fault

> **Issue #993**
> **状态**: closed
> **创建时间**: 2020-01-05T18:33:11Z
> **更新时间**: 2020-01-05T21:06:25Z
> **关闭时间**: 2020-01-05T21:06:25Z
> **作者**: boxerab
> **标签**: 
> **URL**: https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/issues/993

## 描述

Error is:

`Memory access fault by GPU node-1 (Agent handle: 0x555afc5a0640) on address 0x508400000. Reason: Page not present or supervisor privilege.`

Reproducer [here](https://github.com/GrokImageCompression/latke/tree/mem_error) : run the debayer test app.

I have checks in the kernel to make sure that writes are not out of bounds, but I still get an error.
It goes away if I add 1 to the size of the output buffer.



---

## 评论 (6 条)

### 评论 #1 — Moading (2020-01-05T18:45:14Z)

Hi,
I'm hunting a similar bug in my application. I am getting the impression that my bug shows up on Polaris but not on Vega, but that is just a feeling.

The output of 'dmesg' will tell you if your kernel is performing an invalid read or an invalid write.

What hardware (GPU) are you using?

---

### 评论 #2 — boxerab (2020-01-05T19:12:46Z)

Aha! I am also using Polaris - RX 470

---

### 评论 #3 — boxerab (2020-01-05T19:14:56Z)

From `dmesg` :


```
[ 2628.405465] amdgpu 0000:01:00.0: GPU fault detected: 146 0x00028814 for process debayer pid 18204 thread debayer pid 18204
[ 2628.405467] amdgpu 0000:01:00.0:   VM_CONTEXT1_PROTECTION_FAULT_ADDR   0x00508400
[ 2628.405468] amdgpu 0000:01:00.0:   VM_CONTEXT1_PROTECTION_FAULT_STATUS 0x11088014
[ 2628.405469] amdgpu 0000:01:00.0: VM fault (0x14, vmid 8, pasid 32783) at page 5276672, write from 'TC6' (0x54433600) (136)
```

---

### 评论 #4 — Moading (2020-01-05T19:50:58Z)

The access fault happens on Polaris and on Vega after removing the +1 in debayer.cpp.
Here is the output obtained with rocr_debug_agent, maybe that helps.


.../latke-master/build$ ./debayer 


Platform found : Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.

Selected Platform Vendor : Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.
Device 0 : gfx906+sram-ecc Device ID is 0x5595e906f7d0

---

### 评论 #5 — boxerab (2020-01-05T20:00:31Z)

Thanks! So, it does look like a buffer overrun. 

---

### 评论 #6 — boxerab (2020-01-05T21:05:54Z)

So, the issue turned out to be: I am writing out a `uchar3` to a buffer by casting to a pointer, but  `sizeof(uchar3)` actually equals 4, so this is causing a buffer overrun. The assumption was that sizeof would equal 3.

---
